   135â†’
   136â†’== Overview
   137â†’
   138â†’The
   139â†’{jjdgr_gallops}
   140â†’is the persistent data store for Job Jockey.
   141â†’It tracks
   142â†’{jjdhr_heat_s}
   143â†’(bounded initiatives) and their constituent
   144â†’{jjdpr_pace_s}
   145â†’(discrete actions).
   146â†’Each
   147â†’{jjdpr_pace}
   148â†’maintains a history of
   149â†’{jjdkr_tack_s}
   150â†’(plan refinements).
   151â†’
   152â†’== Design Principles
   153â†’
   154â†’=== Exclusive JSON Ownership
   155â†’
   156â†’All access to the
   157â†’{jjdgr_gallops}
   158â†’goes through {jjdx_cli}, the Job Jockey subcommand of the Voce Viva executor.
   159â†’No jq or direct JSON manipulation ever touches this data.
   160â†’This is an absolute architectural constraint, not a preference.
   161â†’
   162â†’The rationale: writing reliable bash with jq pipelines requires fighting LLM training data.
   163â†’The internet is full of bad bash.
   164â†’Rust replaces fragile jq invocations with compiler-enforced correctness.
   165â†’The compiler is tireless; humans are not.
   166â†’
   167â†’=== Git as Journal
   168â†’
   169â†’The
   170â†’{jjdgr_gallops}
   171â†’remains JSON specifically so git can serve as the journalling layer.
   172â†’Every mutation creates a diffable commit.
   173â†’Git owns the complete history; we need not implement our own audit trail.
   174â†’
   175â†’The
   176â†’{jjdkr_tack}
   177â†’history within each
   178â†’{jjdpr_pace}
   179â†’exists for convenient postmortem analysis.
   180â†’Git has the full history, but
   181â†’{jjdkr_tack_s}
   182â†’let us see plan evolution without spelunking commits.
   183â†’
   184â†’=== Diff-Friendly Mutations
   185â†’
   186â†’New
   187â†’{jjdkr_tack_s}
   188â†’prepend to position 0 rather than append.
   189â†’This produces cleaner git diffs: prepending adds a line with trailing comma, while appending would modify the previous last line to add a comma.
   190â†’Small discipline, large cumulative benefit.
   191â†’
   192â†’The
   193â†’{jjdhm_order}
   194â†’array contains full
   195â†’{jjdt_coronet_s}.
   196â†’Each
   197â†’{jjdt_coronet}
   198â†’embeds its parent
   199â†’{jjdt_firemark}
   200â†’(redundant within a single
   201â†’{jjdhr_heat}).
   202â†’This redundancy is cheap and enables grep, copy-paste, and self-documenting JSON.
   203â†’
   204â†’=== Crash-Safe Architecture
   205â†’
   206â†’Bash handles locking via `git update-ref`.
   207â†’{jjdx_cli} owns all
   208â†’{jjdgr_gallops}
   209â†’file access - reading, transforming, and writing.
   210â†’{jjdx_cli} implements atomic writes internally (write to temp file, then rename).
   211â†’If {jjdx_cli} crashes mid-transform, no partial writes occur.
   212â†’
   213â†’The pattern:
   214â†’[source,bash]
   215â†’----
   216â†’lock_acquire
   217â†’vvx jjx gallops nominate --file gallops.json "my-heat" "260111" || { lock_release; die; }
   218â†’lock_release
   219â†’----
   220â†’
   221â†’This keeps bash simple (just locking and invocation) while {jjdx_cli} owns all JSON file operations.
   222â†’
   223â†’=== Deterministic Serialization
   224â†’
   225â†’Implementations MUST serialize JSON object keys in deterministic order to minimize diff churn.
   226â†’For `heats` and `paces` objects, use sorted key order (e.g., Rust `BTreeMap` instead of `HashMap`).
   227â†’This ensures that reordering paces via
   228â†’{jjdo_rail}
   229â†’produces minimal diffs â€” only the `order` array changes, not the entire `paces` object.
   230â†’
   231â†’=== Identity Encoding
   232â†’
   233â†’{jjdt_firemark}
   234â†’(`â‚£` prefix) and
   235â†’{jjdt_coronet}
   236â†’(`â‚¢` prefix) are the canonical identities used in JSON keys, CLI arguments, and display.
   237â†’The base64 encoding provides compact, URL-safe, shell-safe strings.
   238â†’Internally, Rust may decode to integers for arithmetic, but the string form is authoritative.
   239â†’
   240â†’=== Horse Racing Vocabulary
   241â†’
   242â†’Job Jockey uses horse racing metaphors throughout.
   243â†’This is intentional: vivid domain vocabulary helps humans hold complex systems in working memory.
   244â†’
   245â†’* {jjdhr_heat} - a bounded initiative (like a race)
   246â†’* {jjdpr_pace} - a discrete action (the rhythm of the race)
   247â†’* {jjdkr_tack} - plan refinement (equipment; also "tacking" = adjusting course)
   248â†’* {jjdt_firemark} - heat identity, `â‚£`-prefixed (hot-iron brand marking ownership)
   249â†’* {jjdt_coronet} - pace identity, `â‚¢`-prefixed (marking at the hoof line)
   250â†’* {jjdt_silks} - display name (jockey's colors)
   251â†’* {jjdgr_gallops} - the training grounds (where real work happens before race day)
   252â†’
   253â†’== Types
   254â†’
   255â†’[[jjdt_firemark]]
   256â†’// âں¦axl_voices axt_stringâں§
   257â†’{jjdt_firemark}::
   258â†’The identity of a
   259â†’{jjdhr_heat}.
   260â†’Format: `â‚£` + 2 base64 characters (e.g., `â‚£AB`).
   261â†’The 2 base64 characters encode an integer 0-4095 (2^12).
   262â†’Used as JSON keys and in CLI arguments.
   263â†’Input may omit the `â‚£` prefix; output always includes it.
   264â†’
   265â†’[[jjdt_coronet]]
   266â†’// âں¦axl_voices axt_stringâں§
   267â†’{jjdt_coronet}::
   268â†’The identity of a
   269â†’{jjdpr_pace}.
   270â†’Format: `â‚¢` + 5 base64 characters (e.g., `â‚¢ABCDE`).
   271â†’Globally unique: the first 2 base64 characters are the parent
   272â†’{jjdhr_heat_p}
   273â†’encoded identity; the remaining 3 encode the pace index (0-262143, 2^18).
   274â†’Used as JSON keys and in CLI arguments.
   275â†’Input may omit the `â‚¢` prefix; output always includes it.
   276â†’+
   277â†’*Reassignment:* When a
   278â†’{jjdpr_pace}
   279â†’is moved between
   280â†’{jjdhr_heat_s}
   281â†’via
   282â†’{jjdo_draft},
   283â†’the old
   284â†’{jjdt_coronet}
   285â†’becomes invalid and a new one is assigned using the destination
   286â†’{jjdhr_heat_p}
   287â†’{jjdhm_seed}.
   288â†’The new
   289â†’{jjdt_coronet}
   290â†’embeds the destination
   291â†’{jjdt_firemark}.
   292â†’{jjdkr_tack}
   293â†’history is preserved, with a note recording the original
   294â†’{jjdt_coronet}.
   295â†’
   296â†’[[jjdt_silks]]
   297â†’// âں¦axl_voices axt_stringâں§
   298â†’{jjdt_silks}::
   299â†’A kebab-case display name.
   300â†’Used for human-readable identification of
   301â†’{jjdhr_heat_s}
   302â†’and
   303â†’{jjdpr_pace_s}.
   304â†’Pattern: `[a-z0-9]+(-[a-z0-9]+)*`
   305â†’
   306â†’== Records
   307â†’
   308â†’=== {jjdgr_gallops}
   309â†’
   310â†’[[jjdgr_gallops]]
   311â†’// âں¦axl_voices axr_record_jsonâں§
   312â†’{jjdgr_gallops}::
   313â†’The root JSON structure containing all Job Jockey data.
   314â†’Singleton record persisted at `.claude/jjm/jjg_gallops.json`.
   315â†’
   316â†’==== Members
   317â†’
   318â†’* [[jjdgm_seed]]
   319â†’// âں¦axl_voices axr_member axd_requiredâں§
   320â†’{jjdgm_seed}
   321â†’is the next
   322â†’{jjdt_firemark}
   323â†’to allocate, stored as 2 base64 characters.
   324â†’Incremented by
   325â†’{jjdo_nominate}.
   326â†’
   327â†’=== {jjdhr_heat}
   328â†’
   329â†’[[jjdhr_heat]]
   330â†’// âں¦axl_voices axr_record_jsonâں§
   331â†’{jjdhr_heat}::
   332â†’A bounded initiative with coherent goals.
   333â†’Contains zero or more
   334â†’{jjdpr_pace_s}
   335â†’representing discrete actions.
   336â†’Keyed in JSON by
   337â†’{jjdt_firemark}.
   338â†’
   339â†’==== Members
   340â†’
   341â†’* [[jjdhm_firemark]]
   342â†’// âں¦axl_voices axr_member axd_requiredâں§
   343â†’{jjdhm_firemark}
   344â†’is the
   345â†’{jjdhr_heat_p}
   346â†’primary key.
   347â†’Type:
   348â†’{jjdt_firemark}.
   349â†’Implicit from JSON key; not stored as separate field.
   350â†’
   351â†’* [[jjdhm_silks]]
   352â†’// âں¦axl_voices axr_member axd_requiredâں§
   353â†’{jjdhm_silks}
   354â†’is the
   355â†’{jjdhr_heat_p}
   356â†’display name.
   357â†’Type:
   358â†’{jjdt_silks}.
   359â†’
   360â†’* [[jjdhm_created]]
   361â†’// âں¦axl_voices axr_member axd_requiredâں§
   362â†’{jjdhm_created}
   363â†’records when the
   364â†’{jjdhr_heat}
   365â†’was nominated.
   366â†’Format: YYMMDD.
   367â†’
   368â†’* [[jjdhm_status]]
   369â†’// âں¦axl_voices axr_member axd_requiredâں§
   370â†’{jjdhm_status}
   371â†’indicates the
   372â†’{jjdhr_heat_p}
   373â†’lifecycle state.
   374â†’Values:
   375â†’{jjdhe_racing},
   376â†’{jjdhe_stabled},
   377â†’or
   378â†’{jjdhe_retired}.
   379â†’
   380â†’* [[jjdhm_order]]
   381â†’// âں¦axl_voices axr_member axd_required axd_repeatedâں§
   382â†’{jjdhm_order}
   383â†’lists
   384â†’{jjdt_coronet_s}
   385â†’in execution sequence.
   386â†’May be reordered via
   387â†’{jjdo_rail}.
   388â†’
   389â†’* [[jjdhm_seed]]
   390â†’// âں¦axl_voices axr_member axd_requiredâں§
   391â†’{jjdhm_seed}
   392â†’is the next
   393â†’{jjdt_coronet}
   394â†’index to allocate, stored as 3 base64 characters.
   395â†’Incremented by
   396â†’{jjdo_slate}.
   397â†’
   398â†’* [[jjdhm_paddock]]
   399â†’// âں¦axl_voices axr_member axd_requiredâں§
   400â†’{jjdhm_paddock}
   401â†’is the path to the paddock file containing
   402â†’{jjdhr_heat}
   403â†’context and background.
   404â†’Format: `.claude/jjm/jjp_XX.md` where XX is the base64-encoded
   405â†’{jjdt_firemark}
   406â†’(without `â‚£` prefix).
   407â†’Created by
   408â†’{jjdo_nominate}.
   409â†’
   410â†’==== Status Values
   411â†’
   412â†’[[jjdhe_racing]]
   413â†’// âں¦axl_voices axt_enum_valueâں§
   414â†’{jjdhe_racing}::
   415â†’The
   416â†’{jjdhr_heat}
   417â†’is actively being worked.
   418â†’
   419â†’[[jjdhe_stabled]]
   420â†’// âں¦axl_voices axt_enum_valueâں§
   421â†’{jjdhe_stabled}::
   422â†’The
   423â†’{jjdhr_heat}
   424â†’is paused, not actively worked.
   425â†’
   426â†’[[jjdhe_retired]]
   427â†’// âں¦axl_voices axt_enum_valueâں§
   428â†’{jjdhe_retired}::
   429â†’The
   430â†’{jjdhr_heat}
   431â†’is complete and archived.
   432â†’
   433â†’=== {jjdpr_pace}
   434â†’
   435â†’[[jjdpr_pace]]
   436â†’// âں¦axl_voices axr_record_jsonâں§
   437â†’{jjdpr_pace}::
   438â†’A discrete action within a
   439â†’{jjdhr_heat}.
   440â†’Contains one or more
   441â†’{jjdkr_tack_s}
   442â†’representing state snapshots and plan refinements.
   443â†’Current state is derived from `tacks[0].state`.
   444â†’Current silks is derived from `tacks[0].silks`.
   445â†’Keyed in JSON by
   446â†’{jjdt_coronet}.
   447â†’
   448â†’==== Members
   449â†’
   450â†’* [[jjdpm_coronet]]
   451â†’// âں¦axl_voices axr_member axd_requiredâں§
   452â†’{jjdpm_coronet}
   453â†’is the
   454â†’{jjdpr_pace_p}
   455â†’globally unique identity.
   456â†’Type:
   457â†’{jjdt_coronet}.
   458â†’Implicit from JSON key; not stored as separate field.
   459â†’
   460â†’=== {jjdkr_tack}
   461â†’
   462â†’[[jjdkr_tack]]
   463â†’// âں¦axl_voices axr_record_jsonâں§
   464â†’{jjdkr_tack}::
   465â†’A snapshot of
   466â†’{jjdpr_pace}
   467â†’state and plan at a point in time.
   468â†’{jjdkr_tack_s}
   469â†’form an append-only history capturing both plan refinements and state transitions.
   470â†’Position 0 is the most recent; older
   471â†’{jjdkr_tack_s}
   472â†’follow.
   473â†’New
   474â†’{jjdkr_tack_s}
   475â†’are prepended for cleaner git diffs.
   476â†’The current
   477â†’{jjdpr_pace}
   478â†’state is always
   479â†’`tacks[0].state`.
   480â†’
   481â†’==== Members
   482â†’

   600â†’== Serialization
   601â†’
   602â†’[[jjdz_charset]]
   603â†’{jjdz_charset}::
   604â†’The 64-character alphabet for identity encoding.
   605â†’Uses URL-safe base64 (RFC 4648 آ§5):
   606â†’`ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz0123456789-_`
   607â†’Position in charset determines numeric value (A=0, B=1, ..., `-`=62, `_`=63).
   608â†’This alphabet is safe for JSON keys, URLs, shell, git refs, and filenames.
   609â†’Matches Rust `base64::engine::general_purpose::URL_SAFE_NO_PAD`.
   610â†’
   611â†’[[jjdz_encoding]]
   612â†’{jjdz_encoding}::
   613â†’The algorithm for encoding
   614â†’{jjdt_firemark}
   615â†’and
   616â†’{jjdt_coronet}
   617â†’values.
   618â†’+
   619â†’*{jjdt_firemark} (Heat identity):*
   620â†’+
   621â†’. Format: `â‚£` + 2 base64 characters
   622â†’. Given integer H (0-4095): encode as `â‚£` + `charset[H/64]` + `charset[H%64]`
   623â†’. To decode `â‚£XY`: H = pos(X) * 64 + pos(Y)
   624â†’+
   625â†’*{jjdt_coronet} (Pace identity):*
   626â†’+
   627â†’. Format: `â‚¢` + 5 base64 characters
   628â†’. First 2 characters encode the parent Heat (same as {jjdt_firemark} without prefix)
   629â†’. Last 3 characters encode the pace index P (0-262143)
   630â†’. Given Heat H and pace index P: encode as `â‚¢` + `charset[H/64]` + `charset[H%64]` + `charset[P/4096]` + `charset[(P/64)%64]` + `charset[P%64]`
   631â†’. To decode `â‚¢XYZAB`: H from XY, P = pos(Z) * 4096 + pos(A) * 64 + pos(B)
   632â†’+
   633â†’*Input flexibility:*
   634â†’+
   635â†’CLI commands accept identities with or without prefix.
   636â†’Length determines type: 2 base64 chars = {jjdt_firemark}, 5 base64 chars = {jjdt_coronet}.
   637â†’Output always includes the prefix (`â‚£` or `â‚¢`).

   843â†’== Commit Message Architecture
   844â†’
   845â†’All JJ commits include an action code that serves multiple purposes:
   846â†’- **Parsing steeplechase history**: Distinguishes between different operation types
   847â†’- **Semantic clarity**: Indicates the nature and context of each commit
   848â†’- **Consistency**: Applies uniformly across pace-level and heat-level operations
   849â†’
   850â†’== Routines
   851â†’
   852â†’Routines are internal reusable procedures invoked by operations.
   853â†’They encapsulate common logic for consistent behavior across the codebase.
   854â†’
   855â†’[[jjdr_load]]
   856â†’// âں¦axl_voices axo_routineâں§
   857â†’=== {jjdr_load}
   858â†’
   859â†’Load and validate the
   860â†’{jjdgr_gallops}
   861â†’file.
   862â†’Every operation that reads
   863â†’{jjdgr_gallops}
   864â†’invokes this routine.
   865â†’Returns a validated
   866â†’{jjdgr_gallops}
   867â†’only after all checks pass.
   868â†’
   869â†’{jjds_arguments}
   870â†’
   871â†’// âں¦axd_requiredâں§
   872â†’* {jjda_file}
   873â†’
   874â†’{jjds_behavior}
   875â†’
   876â†’. Read file contents as raw bytes
   877â†’. Deserialize bytes into
   878â†’{jjdgr_gallops}
   879â†’structure
   880â†’. Reserialize
   881â†’{jjdgr_gallops}
   882â†’structure to JSON string
   883â†’. Compare reserialized bytes against original bytes
   884â†’. If different:
   885â†’{axc_fatal}
   886â†’with diagnostic showing first difference location
   887â†’. Validate semantic constraints (timestamps, kebab-case silks, invariants)
   888â†’. If validation fails:
   889â†’{axc_fatal}
   890â†’with validation errors
   891â†’. Return validated
   892â†’{jjdgr_gallops}
   893â†’
   894â†’{jjds_stdout} None (returns structure to caller)
   895â†’
   896â†’{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero on validation failure or I/O error.
   897â†’
   898â†’*Architectural constraint:*
   899â†’{jjdr_load}
   900â†’is the ONLY way to obtain a
   901â†’{jjdgr_gallops}
   902â†’from disk.
   903â†’This makes validation bypass architecturally impossible.
   904â†’The round-trip check (steps 3-5) ensures the file is in canonical form.
   905â†’The semantic validation (steps 6-7) ensures business rules are satisfied.
   906â†’
   907â†’[[jjdr_save]]
   908â†’// âں¦axl_voices axo_routineâں§
   909â†’=== {jjdr_save}
   910â†’
   911â†’Persist
   912â†’{jjdgr_gallops}
   913â†’to disk with validation.
   914â†’Every operation that writes
   915â†’{jjdgr_gallops}
   916â†’invokes this routine.
   917â†’Validates output by loading it back before finalizing.
   918â†’
   919â†’{jjds_arguments}
   920â†’
   921â†’// âں¦axd_requiredâں§
   922â†’*
   923â†’{jjdgr_gallops}
   924â†’(in-memory structure to persist)
   925â†’
   926â†’// âں¦axd_requiredâں§
   927â†’* {jjda_file}
   928â†’
   929â†’{jjds_behavior}
   930â†’
   931â†’. Serialize
   932â†’{jjdgr_gallops}
   933â†’to canonical JSON string
   934â†’. Write JSON to temporary file (atomic write preparation)
   935â†’. Call
   936â†’{jjdr_load}
   937â†’on temporary file to validate what was written
   938â†’. If
   939â†’{jjdr_load}
   940â†’fails: delete temporary file,
   941â†’{axc_fatal}
   942â†’with error
   943â†’. Rename temporary file to final path (atomic commit)
   944â†’
   945â†’{jjds_stdout} None
   946â†’
   947â†’{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero on validation failure or I/O error.
   948â†’
   949â†’*Design rationale:*
   950â†’{jjdr_save}
   951â†’reuses
   952â†’{jjdr_load}
   953â†’for validation, ensuring a single validation code path for both reading and writing.
   954â†’If an operation produces invalid output, the save fails before corrupting the file.
   955â†’
   956â†’[[jjdr_persist]]
   957â†’// âں¦axl_voices axo_routineâں§
   958â†’=== {jjdr_persist}
   959â†’
   960â†’Persist
   961â†’{jjdgr_gallops}
   962â†’state and commit changes to git with a JJ-aware commit message.
   963â†’This routine combines
   964â†’{jjdr_save}
   965â†’with git commit operations to maintain the journalling discipline.
   966â†’Used by most write operations to ensure every
   967â†’{jjdgr_gallops}
   968â†’mutation is tracked in git history.
   969â†’
   970â†’{jjds_arguments}
   971â†’

   973â†’* lock (lock handle) â€” acquired lock from `vvg_lock_acquire`
   974â†’

   977â†’{jjdgr_gallops}

   979â†’
   980â†’// âں¦axd_requiredâں§
   981â†’* {jjda_file}

   984â†’* identity â€”
   985â†’{jjdt_firemark}
   986â†’for heat-level operations or

   992â†’
   993â†’// âں¦axd_requiredâں§

   995â†’
   996â†’{jjds_behavior}
   997â†’
   998â†’.
   999â†’{jjdr_save}
  1000â†’{jjdgr_gallops}
  1001â†’to
  1002â†’{jjda_file}
  1003â†’. Stage gallops file with `git add {file}`
  1004â†’. If paddock file exists for this identity, stage it with `git add {paddock_file}`
  1005â†’. Run size guard check with provided size_limit
  1006â†’. Determine action code:
  1007â†’.. If identity is
  1008â†’{jjdt_firemark}:
  1009â†’extract from message prefix (`N:`, `S:`, `r:`, `T:`, `D:`, `R:`)
  1010â†’.. If identity is
  1011â†’{jjdt_coronet}:
  1012â†’extract from message prefix (`n:`, `A:`, `W:`, `F:`, `d:`)
  1013â†’. Format full commit message: `jjb:HALLMARK:IDENTITY:ACTION: message\n\nCo-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>`
  1014â†’. Run `git commit -m "..."`
  1015â†’. Return commit hash
  1016â†’
  1017â†’{jjds_stdout} Commit hash (returned to caller)
  1018â†’
  1019â†’{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero on error.

  1043â†’*Commands that do NOT use
  1044â†’{jjdr_persist}:*
  1045â†’
  1046â†’*
  1047â†’{jjdo_retire}
  1048â†’â€” uses custom commit logic for trophy archival (stages trophy file, gallops, and paddock deletion; uses 200KB limit)
  1049â†’*
  1050â†’{jjdo_chalk}

  1055â†’
  1056â†’*Design rationale:*
  1057â†’{jjdr_persist}
  1058â†’ensures consistent commit message formatting and git discipline across all
  1059â†’{jjdgr_gallops}
  1060â†’mutations.
  1061â†’By centralizing the commit logic, we guarantee that every state change is properly journalled with parseable commit messages for

  1065â†’[[jjdr_wrap]]
  1066â†’// âں¦axl_voices axo_routineâں§
  1067â†’=== {jjdr_wrap}
  1068â†’
  1069â†’Mark a pace complete and commit all changes in one self-contained operation.
  1070â†’This routine combines staging, committing, state transition, and chalk marker creation.
  1071â†’Used by `/jjc-pace-wrap` to provide a single-command wrap experience.
  1072â†’
  1073â†’{jjds_arguments}
  1074â†’
  1075â†’// âں¦axd_requiredâں§
  1076â†’* {jjdt_coronet}
  1077â†’(positional) â€” pace to wrap
  1078â†’
  1079â†’// âں¦axd_optionalâں§
  1080â†’* `--size-limit` â€” maximum commit size in bytes (default: 50KB)
  1081â†’
  1082â†’{jjds_behavior}
  1083â†’
  1084â†’. Acquire lock (`refs/vvg/locks/vvx`)
  1085â†’. Stage all changes (`git add -A`)
  1086â†’. Size guard check:
  1087â†’.. Get staged content size
  1088â†’.. If over limit: release lock, exit 2
  1089â†’. Generate commit message via Claude from diff
  1090â†’. Commit staged changes
  1091â†’. Transition pace state to complete via
  1092â†’{jjdo_tally}
  1093â†’. Create W chalk marker with outcome summary
  1094â†’. Release lock
  1095â†’. Return commit hash
  1096â†’
  1097â†’{jjds_exit_enumerated}
  1098â†’
  1099â†’* 0 â€” success
  1100â†’* 1 â€” general error (parse failure, gallops error, git error)
  1101â†’* 2 â€” size guard exceeded (use --size-limit to override)
  1102â†’
  1103â†’*Design rationale:*
  1104â†’{jjdr_wrap}
  1105â†’consolidates the multi-step wrap workflow into a single vvx command.
  1106â†’This eliminates the need for slash commands to orchestrate separate notch, tally, and chalk operations.
  1107â†’The distinct exit code 2 for size guard allows callers to advise users about the --size-limit flag.
  1108â†’
  1109â†’== Operations
  1110â†’
  1111â†’Operations are implemented as {jjdx_cli} subcommands.
  1112â†’{jjdx_cli} owns all
  1113â†’{jjdgr_gallops}
  1114â†’file access through
  1115â†’{jjdr_load}
  1116â†’and
  1117â†’{jjdr_save}.
  1118â†’Bash handles locking via `git update-ref` and invokes {jjdx_cli} with
  1119â†’{jjda_file}.

  2554â†’=== Steeplechase Operations
  2555â†’
  2556â†’The steeplechase is Job Jockey's session history, stored in git commits rather than the
  2557â†’{jjdgr_gallops}
  2558â†’JSON.
  2559â†’Commit messages follow patterns that
  2560â†’{jjdo_rein}
  2561â†’can parse to reconstruct session history.
  2562â†’
  2563â†’==== Commit Message Patterns
  2564â†’
  2565â†’All JJ commits use the format: `jjb:HALLMARK:IDENTITY:ACTION: message`
  2566â†’
  2567â†’* `jjb` â€” JJ brand prefix (literal)
  2568â†’* `HALLMARK` â€” Version identifier. Format: NNNN (installed) or NNNN-xxxxxxx (dev). Source: `.vvk/vvbf_brand.json` (installed) or max hallmark from `Tools/vok/vov_veiled/vovr_registry.json` + git HEAD (Kit Forge)
  2569â†’* `IDENTITY` â€” â‚¢CORONET for pace-level, â‚£FIREMARK for heat-level
  2570â†’* `ACTION` â€” single-letter code (required for all commits)
  2571â†’
  2572â†’*Standard notch* (created by {jjdo_notch}):
  2573â†’----
  2574â†’jjb:HALLMARK:â‚¢XXXXX:n: commit message
  2575â†’
  2576â†’Co-Authored-By: ...
  2577â†’----
  2578â†’
  2579â†’Standard notch commits use `n` to denote routine progress tracking.
  2580â†’
  2581â†’*Pace-level chalk* (created by {jjdo_chalk}):
  2582â†’----
  2583â†’jjb:HALLMARK:â‚¢XXXXX:A: description
  2584â†’
  2585â†’Co-Authored-By: ...
  2586â†’----
  2587â†’
  2588â†’Pace-level action codes:
  2589â†’
  2590â†’* `n` â€” Notch: standard commit
  2591â†’* `A` â€” APPROACH: proposed approach before work begins
  2592â†’* `W` â€” WRAP: pace completion summary
  2593â†’* `F` â€” FLY: autonomous execution began (bridled pace)
  2594â†’* `d` â€” discussion: significant decision (lowercase, can also be heat-level)
  2595â†’
  2596â†’*Heat-level operations*:
  2597â†’----
  2598â†’jjb:HALLMARK:â‚£XX:N: heat-silks
  2599â†’jjb:HALLMARK:â‚£XX:S: pace-silks
  2600â†’jjb:HALLMARK:â‚£XX:r: reordered
  2601â†’jjb:HALLMARK:â‚£XX:T: pace-silks
  2602â†’jjb:HALLMARK:â‚£XX:D: old-coronet â†’ new-coronet
  2603â†’jjb:HALLMARK:â‚£XX:R: heat-silks
  2604â†’----
  2605â†’
  2606â†’Heat-level action codes:
  2607â†’
  2608â†’* `N` â€” Nominate: create new heat
  2609â†’* `S` â€” Slate: add new pace
  2610â†’* `r` â€” rail: reorder paces (lowercase)
  2611â†’* `T` â€” Tally: add tack to pace
  2612â†’* `D` â€” Draft: move pace between heats (uppercase, rare)
  2613â†’* `R` â€” Retire: archive heat (uppercase)
  2614â†’

  2620â†’{jjdhr_heat}.
  2621â†’
  2622â†’{jjds_arguments}

  2629â†’// âں¦axd_optionalâں§

  2631â†’
  2632â†’{jjds_stdout} JSON array of steeplechase entries:
  2633â†’[source,json]
  2634â†’----
  2635â†’[
  2636â†’  {
  2637â†’    "timestamp": "YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM",
  2638â†’    "commit": "abc123ef",
  2639â†’    "hallmark": "1009",
  2640â†’    "coronet": "â‚¢XXXXX",

  2642â†’    "subject": "commit message"
  2643â†’  },
  2644â†’  {
  2645â†’    "timestamp": "YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM",
  2646â†’    "commit": "def456ab",
  2647â†’    "hallmark": "1009-abc1234",
  2648â†’    "coronet": "â‚¢XXXXX",

  2660â†’Field `hallmark` is always present.

  2662â†’Field `action` is present for all commits, including standard notch commits (`n`).

  2665â†’{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.

  2667â†’{jjds_behavior}
  2668â†’
  2669â†’. Run `git log --all --extended-regexp --grep="^jjb:[^:]+:(â‚£XX|â‚¢XX)" --format="%ai%x09%s"`
  2670â†’  (skips hallmark field; filtering is by identity)
  2671â†’. Parse each line: extract timestamp, hallmark, identity, action, and message
  2672â†’. For pace-level (â‚¢CORONET): verify coronet belongs to target heat
  2673â†’. For heat-level (â‚£FIREMARK): verify firemark matches target heat
  2674â†’. Build JSON array, limit to requested count
  2675â†’. Output JSON

  2678â†’// âں¦axl_voices axi_cli_subcommandâں§
  2679â†’==== {jjdo_notch}
  2680â†’
  2681â†’Create a JJ-aware git commit with heat/pace context prefix.
  2682â†’
  2683â†’{jjds_arguments}
  2684â†’
  2685â†’// âں¦axd_requiredâں§
  2686â†’* identity (positional) â€” {jjdt_coronet} for pace-affiliated commit, or {jjdt_firemark} for heat-only commit
  2687â†’
  2688â†’// âں¦axd_requiredâں§
  2689â†’* files (positional, repeated) â€” paths to commit (at least one required)
  2690â†’
  2691â†’// âں¦axd_optionalâں§
  2692â†’* `--size-limit` â€” size limit in bytes for the commit guard (default: 50KB)
  2693â†’
  2694â†’{jjds_stdout} Commit hash
  2695â†’
  2696â†’{jjds_exit_uniform} 0 success, non-zero error.
  2697â†’
  2698â†’{jjds_behavior}
  2699â†’
  2700â†’. Parse identity:
  2701â†’.. If 5 base64 chars (Coronet): pace-affiliated commit
  2702â†’.. If 2 base64 chars (Firemark): heat-only commit (no pace affiliation)
  2703â†’. Validate file list:
  2704â†’.. If empty: exit 1 with "error: no files specified"
  2705â†’.. If any file does not exist: exit 1 with "error: file not found: {path}"
  2706â†’. Check for uncommitted changes outside file list:
  2707â†’.. Run `git status --porcelain` to get all modified files
  2708â†’.. For each modified file NOT in the specified list, warn to stderr:
  2709â†’+
  2710â†’----
  2711â†’warning: uncommitted changes outside file list:
  2712â†’  modified: path/to/file.rs
  2713â†’----
  2714â†’. Acquire lock (`refs/vvg/locks/vvx`)
  2715â†’. Stage specified files only (`git add <file1> <file2> ...`)
  2716â†’. Run size guard check (uses `--size-limit` if provided, otherwise 50KB default)
  2717â†’. Run `claude --print "Generate commit message for this diff: $(git diff --cached)"`
  2718â†’. Format full message based on identity type:
  2719â†’.. Coronet: `jjb:HALLMARK:â‚¢XXXXX:n: message\n\nCo-Authored-By: ...`
  2720â†’.. Firemark: `jjb:HALLMARK:â‚£XX:n: message\n\nCo-Authored-By: ...`
  2721â†’. Run `git commit -m "..."`
  2722â†’. Release lock
